title: Report
description: The canonical shape of a Reports record — docs/29-PHASE-2A-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §4/§8's already-locked sheet and architecture, and the concrete storage of docs/33-DOMAIN-MODEL.md §3.3's Report. Backs the patient-facing Reports dashboard card (upload control + recent uploads) and full history page (docs/29 §5). Binary content itself lives in Google Drive, keyed by drive_file_id — this schema is metadata only. See report.md and docs/42-REPORTS-UPLOAD-READINESS-REVIEW.md for the create-only lifecycle, the authorization model, and the disclosed MIME-validation limitation.

record_id.description: The permanent identity of this entry. A UUID, generated once at creation, never reused or changed, SQL-migration-safe by design (ADR-006). The only field a client-supplied download/list request identifies a report by — never file_name or list position. Also the Drive object's own filename (docs/42 §5) — never the patient-supplied filename.

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this report belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Always session-derived at write time and re-verified at every read/download time — never accepted from the request body.

uploaded_at.description: Server-set at write time (foundationNowIso_()), never patient-editable. This entry's sole sort key (descending) for the full history list.

file_name.description: The original, client-supplied filename, kept for display purposes only. Never used as a Drive object name or any path component (docs/29 §8, docs/42 §5) — the actual stored Drive file is named from record_id alone, never from this field and never from patient_id. Escaped for display wherever rendered; never trusted as markup.

drive_file_id.description: The Google Drive file ID holding the actual binary content. Drive's own sharing permission on this file is explicitly set to private/no-access (docs/42 §6's 'single most important property') as defense-in-depth only — it is never the authorization boundary; every list/download call re-verifies patient_id against the session before this ID is ever used to touch Drive.

mime_type.description: The server-detected content type — Utilities.newBlob()'s byte-structure-based detection of the actual decoded upload bytes, not the client-declared File.type (docs/29 §8, shared/constants/upload-limits.md's disclosed content-sniffing limitation). One of upload-limits.json's allowed_file_types entries.

size_bytes.description: The real, server-measured length of the decoded upload in bytes — never the client-reported size. Enforced at write time against shared/constants/upload-limits.json's max_upload_bytes.

uploaded_by.description: Who performed this upload: the session-derived patient_id for the patient-facing upload route (self-upload is the normal case), or a staff identifier string for the manually-run staff wrapper (docs/33 §3.3's "patient or staff" — no staff Web App route exists, per the approved architecture decision; a staff-attributed row can only be written by running createFoundationReportForExistingDriveFile() from the Apps Script editor).
